seniority.level: Staff / Lead IC (likely equivalent to Senior PM or Principal PM)
level_signals: Comp range $204K-$240K base in SF/NY, consistent with senior/lead IC
Expected to drive strategy, prioritization, and product delivery independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 7+ years experience in product management in a high-paced, agile software development environment, with a proven track record of shipping impactful products.
You have several years of experience in building products at startups in cross-functional teams with product designers, engineers, and business partners.
You are empathic and driven by solving problems for your users. You have the ability to dig deep into their problems and really understand what drives them.
You are a systems thinker and solve problems in a holistic way. You draw data flow diagrams in your sleep and complexity makes you salivate.
You traverse between altitudes with ease. You can think big picture on product strategy in one moment and then get in the trenches to solve a calculation definition issue in the next.

You are resourceful and scrappy in the right ways. You know when to invest versus stand-up fake doors to validate a hypothesis. You understand that sometimes the best path forward may not be through building software.

scope.surface: Carta platform — market-leading and transformative offerings across equity ownership, fund administration, and private markets ERP
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise software for private markets (fund administration, equity management)
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption and retention across 65,000+ companies
core_responsibilities: Drive strategy, prioritization, and product delivery
Build intuitive solutions to complex problems
Work across the platform to ensure offerings remain market-leading and transformative
Impact how ownership is managed and distributed around the world

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full time roles

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($204K-$240K base in SF/NY/Santa Clara)
Equity included for all full-time roles
Clear company mission and product surface (private markets ERP)
Large, established customer base (65,000+ companies, $185B AUM) signals product scale
Recognized by Fortune, Forbes, Fast Company — strong employer brand
red_flags: Team assignment is deferred to final stage interview — candidate may not know which team they join until late in the process
No explicit visa sponsorship information stated; E-Verify mention implies US work authorization required
Location type is ambiguous — JD lists specific city salary bands implying onsite/hybrid but does not explicitly state remote eligibility
Role scope is vague ('work across our platform') — specific product area ownership is unclear until late-stage interview

ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite. The presence of city-specific salary tiers (SF, Santa Clara, NY, Seattle) suggests location-tied roles, treated as onsite here.
Visa sponsorship is unclear. E-Verify usage implies US employment authorization required, but whether sponsorship is offered is not stated.
'Several years' for startup experience is unquantified — treated as a hard requirement given the unconditional framing but minimum value left null.
